tier2.analysis: EIX presents a compelling value case with a very low PE of 6.36 and a strong dividend yield of 4.78%, suggesting the stock is attractively priced relative to earnings. The stock hitting a 52-week high at $63.21 signals positive momentum and renewed investor confidence. Upbeat 2026-27 guidance combined with AI grid infrastructure investment thesis adds a meaningful growth catalyst, positioning EIX well for utility sector tailwinds driven by rising power demand. VIX at 23.87 indicates moderate market anxiety, which typically benefits defensive utility names. The scan score of 10.3 reinforces algorithmic bullishness. Key risks include wildfire liability exposure in California and regulatory headwinds, which partly explain the depressed PE multiple. Overall, fundamentals are undervalued, sentiment is constructive, and the AI/grid narrative provides a secular growth overlay.
